name: Heaven and Hell Cake
recipeInstructions:
To make the angel food:
1. Heat oven to 375°F.
2. Cut parchment or wax paper to fit the bottom of a 10-inch round cake pan. Do not grease the pan or paper.
3. Sift together the flour and confectioners' sugar. Set aside.
4. Place egg whites in bowl of heavy-duty mixer. Beat slowly while adding salt and cream of tartar, then continue beating for 1 minute or until soft peaks form.
5. Increase speed to medium; add sugar by tablespoons until all incorporated, then beat 1 1/2 minutes longer.
6. When egg whites have stiff peaks, add vanilla and almond extracts.
7. Remove bowl from mixer and sprinkle half of the confectioners' sugar-flour mixture over the egg whites. Fold with a rubber spatula; sprinkle with remaining sugar-flour mixture and fold again using a minimum number of strokes so that the egg whites do not deflate.
8. Gently spoon the mixture into prepared pan and bake 40 to 50 minutes, or until golden brown. Do not overbake or the cake will sink in the center.
To make the devil's food:
1. Heat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and flour a round 10-inch cake pan.
3. Sift cocoa into mixing bowl, then drizzle coffee while whisking to make smooth paste. Set aside.
4. Combine shortening, sugar, vanilla, and eggs in bowl; beat for 2 minutes at medium speed.
5. In separate bowl, sift together flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.
6. Alternately add cocoa-coffee mixture and flour mixture to sugar-egg mixture and continue beating until incorporated. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake 30 minutes.
To assemble cake:
1. When both cakes have cooled, carefully slice them in half horizontally with sharp serrated knife (longer the better) so you have 4 cake layers.
2. To slice angel food cake, spray knife with oil. Hold top of cake with flat open hand and use sawing action to slice through. Do not put pressure on knife or cake might tear; let sawing action do its work. If cake sinks in center, fill with extra mousse. Make this the top layer and it will be easier to level off.
3. Place one layer on devil's food cake on plate and spoon third of Peanut Butter Mousse on top. Add second layer of devil's food cake and spoon third of Peanut Butter Mousse on top. Add angel food layer, spreading with another third of mousse. Add second devil's food layer and remaining mousse.
4. Top with remaining angel food layer.
5. Whisk ganache and spread over cake generously with spatula. Frost top and sides. Refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving. Served chilled, slice with warm wet knife.
